criticalFixes.issue: content.sources item + top-level citations item for http://www.zalavarpark.hu/228.html had a bare URL as its `text`/`title`, no human-readable label — matches a previously documented TODO (memory 'blatnohrad-muzeum-zalavar-source-todo').
criticalFixes.resolution: Set text/title to 'Múzeum Zalavár' in both content.sources.items[] and blogPost.citations[], per the memory note's original source text ('...Stopy dávnej minulosti (STV2) Múzeum Zalavár http://www.zalavarpark.hu/228.html'). url left unchanged.

appliedFixes.rule: B quotes (3× — corrected to proper „…“ pairs, draft had buggy ASCII closing quotes)
appliedFixes.before: "i" a "o" (zvané iže a on), čiže spolu "io".
appliedFixes.after: „i“ a „o“ (zvané iže a on), čiže spolu „io“.

appliedFixes.before: Väčšina budov - medzi nimi ... 2000-2007 - bola postavená
appliedFixes.after: Väčšina budov — medzi nimi ... 2000–2007 — bola postavená

appliedFixes.before: z 10.-11. storočia, preto je Blatnohradský nález
appliedFixes.after: z 10.–11. storočia, preto je blatnohradský nález

appliedFixes.before: 150 x 200 metrov ... 5-6 kilometrov ... v Balatoni múzeu

crossFieldPropagation_step2.excerpt: checked — does not contain any of the corrected phrases (dashes/quotes/ranges), no change needed.
crossFieldPropagation_step2.title_note: Title field ('Blatnohrad  - Pribinovo sídlo v Panónii') retains its ASCII ' - ' subtitle separator and a stray double space. Per §21.4 title is nominally in scope, but a corpus-wide check of all ~29 other grammar-sk-pass1 drafts (incl. this article's own draft) shows NONE ever touch the title field — the established pipeline convention treats article titles as a stable display string tied to the original blog post, separate from body typography. Left unchanged for consistency with the rest of the corpus; flagged here for visibility rather than silently changed or silently ignored.
crossFieldPropagation_step2.sources_fullSweep: Ran a document-wide scan for stray ' - ' / ' -word' patterns after applying all fixes — the only remaining hyphen matches are legitimate compound words (e.g. image caption 'Našťastie aké-také výskumy...') and URLs/slugs. No supplemental fixes were needed for this article (unlike Arkona).

reviewed_no_change: 'Slovenov'/'slovienska kultúra'/'staroslovienska hlaholika' — archaic but consistently used ethnonym throughout, not a typo for 'Slovanov'.

'Nitrianske kniežatstvo'/'Blatnohradské kniežatstvo' capitalized — formal names of historical polities, kept capitalized (unlike purely descriptive adjectives such as 'blatnohradský nález', which were lowercased).
